dialogue:act2-coastal-kiosk.speaker: Map Kiosk
pages: WELCOME TO THE BRIGHT SIDE. THE SERVICE SEAM IS NOT LISTED ON THIS MAP.
Bosch can feel the missing lane more clearly than the route the kiosk is trying to sell him.

dialogue:archive-console-link.speaker: Bosch Terminal
pages: Bosch, the route is not neutral. A public version of you is already moving through town ahead of your body, and town services are starting to treat him as convenient.
Ask where the pressure starts or why the town needs a Bosch it can process.

interior:bosch-bedroom-v0.speaker: Biscuit
pages: Biscuit holds up their phone like it is too hot to keep.
" The Route Brief on the desk is already open, and the timestamp says it opened from outside the house while Bosch was
asleep. The checklist is rude but clear: witness, proof service, parks, Station Gate.
It feels less like homework and more like a town trying to file a version of Bosch before Bosch can object.

interior:bosch-main-room-v0.speaker: Biscuit
pages: Downstairs, the house keeps pretending to be a house.
The TV is muted on a civic route alert, the table mail has Bosch's name spelled with a question mark, and every phone is
trying not to buzz. Biscuit says the sidewalk kid saw the other Bosch first.
The entry bowl gives off a small warm click, like it heard that and wants Bosch to leave with something the street can
recognize.

interior:bosch-side-room-v0.speaker: Bosch
pages: Behind the storage boxes: the Route Brief backup, a Practice Bat, and a Public Version Card with Bosch's smile printed a
little wrong. The bat is something solid. The card is something social.
The backup brief has three margin notes in handwriting Bosch does not recognize: keep your proof warm, keep your route
local, do not let a crowd finish your sentence. Together they feel like a starter kit for arguing with a town.

interior:bosch-upstairs-hall-v0.speaker: Biscuit
pages: Biscuit blocks the upstairs landing just long enough to point at the side room.
Downstairs, three phones buzz and stop at the exact same time.
"If your public version is already outside, bring something real from inside. Weird guide rule. "

interior:burger-shop-v0.speaker: Morrow
pages: Morrow at the counter is reading from a flyer aloud: "Swag is eternal. SOURCE?
" The kid does not notice Bosch is in the room until the fryer timer beeps in the rhythm of his name.
The grill line keeps humming because local businesses survive by not making eye contact with omens.

interior:corner-shop-v0.speaker: Sal
pages: Sal slides Bosch's Public Version Card under the Tojiba kiosk scanner, then stops the machine before it finishes the
sentence. "Hot Wallet mint, witness packet, park route.
" The kiosk spits out a proof token, a warm charm echo, and a gum coupon stamped NOT ID.

interior:home-hall-v0.speaker: Biscuit
pages: " A flyer has slid through the mail slot: Bosch's face cropped wrong, the quote printed below.
The back is stamped VESSEL, like the house accepted delivery for a version of Bosch nobody ordered.

interior:neighbor-house-v0.speaker: Bonkle
pages: Bonkle keeps one hand on the chain and one eye on the sidewalk. "Other-you had your face and a cleaner story.
Said 'Swag is eternal' like he found it folded in somebody else's pocket.
I repeated it once and heard City Hall start making room for a stamp. "

target:bosch-main-room-v0:cousin.speaker: Cousin
pages: Cousin peels a half-stuck sticker off their sleeve: SOURCE? in marker, then a tiny arrow pointing at Bosch.
"The road is doing that fake-helpful thing. "

target:bosch-main-room-v0:guardian.speaker: Guardian
pages: Guardian has three receipt slips clipped to a fridge magnet and none of them agree.
"Bosch, take the Proof-Token Charm before you let the sidewalk count you. I packed snacks, not destiny. "

target:bosch-main-room-v0:proof-token-bo.speaker: Entry Bowl
pages: Bosch takes the Proof-Token Charm. It is not money, not a weapon, and not permission.
It is a small warm proof that the name and sprite leaving the house belong to the same person.
For one second it reflects the room wrong: a bowl, a doorway, a sidewalk witness, and a version of Bosch that never
learned how to wait.

target:bosch-main-room-v0:receipt-router.speaker: Receipt Router
pages: The Receipt Router coughs, prints a crooked strip, and offers three household options: Sync the morning, check Status,
or pretend this is normal. A taped note says: LOCAL PROOF ONLY. NOT A PORTAL. NOT A PARENT.

target:bosch-main-room-v0:signal-pet-gat.speaker: Signal-Pet
pages: The signal-pet presses one paw against the south path.
It does not bark; it listens to the room like a radio under a blanket.
When the Proof-Token Charm warms in Bosch's hand, it sniffs once, accepts the match, and shuffles aside.
The house has approved the person leaving. The town still has to be convinced.
